text.title: C# ASP.NET Core + EF Core Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules grounding production ASP.NET Core + EF Core feature folders.

rule.statement: Every HTTP controller MUST carry `[ApiController]` + `[Route("api/[controller]")]` and inherit `ControllerBase` (not `Controller`). Route parameters MUST be typed (`int id`, `Guid orderId`), not `string`.

rationale.source: Microsoft Learn — Create web APIs with ASP.NET Core

rule.rationale: `[ApiController]` auto-validates `ModelState`, infers binding sources, and produces RFC 7807 `ProblemDetails` on 400/500. `Controller` brings View support not needed for APIs.

rule.statement: `DbContext` MUST be registered scoped (`AddDbContext` or `AddDbContextPool`). Services that need it MUST be scoped or transient — never singleton.
rule.rationale: `DbContext` is not thread-safe; singleton capture leaks connections and corrupts change-tracking across requests.

rule.statement: Every csproj MUST set `<Nullable>enable</Nullable>` and `<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>` for CS86xx nullable warnings. Public APIs MUST annotate nullability.
rule.rationale: Disabled nullable refs cause silent `NullReferenceException`. CI must fail on `CS8600/CS8601/CS8602/CS8603/CS8604`.

rule.statement: Transport DTOs (request/response models) MUST be `record` (or `record struct` for small immutable shapes) — never mutable `class`. Domain entities live in a separate namespace and MUST NOT be returned by controllers.
rule.rationale: `record` gives value equality, `with`-expressions, and immutability for free; using entities as DTOs leaks persistence concerns into the public API.

rule.statement: Read-only queries that project to DTO/anonymous types MUST use `.AsNoTracking()`. `IQueryable<Entity>` returned to controllers is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Tracking adds memory + CPU overhead and risks lazy-loaded properties firing inside JSON serialisation; returning `IQueryable` leaks query execution to the response pipeline.
